V. LLM Review (30pts).5.1 Algorithm correctness (12): 6/12 — Implements the basic accept/reject idea and does resample from clamp(p_target - p_draft, 0) normalized. However the rejection-sampling math is not lossless as written for general batching: acceptance is decided with `.all().item()` across the batch, forcing all sequences to accept/reject together (incorrect unless B==1). Uses an auxiliary RNG for acceptance coins (not the standard algorithm requirement; changes the joint randomness vs baseline and can affect distributional equivalence tests). Temperature==0 path uses `accepted = (pt_x > 0)` which is not the correct greedy/speculative acceptance rule; it should deterministically accept only if the draft token equals target argmax (or equivalently pt_x==1 under a true one-hot target), but here target probs are one-hot while draft probs may also be one-hot, making behavior fragile. Also uses eps in ratio denominator, which can slightly bias acceptance when pd_x is tiny but nonzero.
V. LLM Review (30pts).5.2 Engineering quality (10): 7/10 — Target verification is done in a single forward pass over all uncached tokens (good). KV rollback is implemented via slicing views (good). However the main token sequence construction repeatedly uses `torch.cat` on `idx` each token/rollback, causing reallocations and extra copies (inefficient). Draft cache handling is inconsistent: comment notes draft_past is 'behind by 1 token' each round and relies on catch-up; works but is awkward and can add extra forward passes. Batchwise `.all()` accept/reject also prevents efficient per-sequence control flow.
V. LLM Review (30pts).5.3 Completeness and robustness (8): 6/8 — Provides both baseline autoregressive and speculative decoding, supports gamma, and has temperature==0 and >0 modes. But temperature==0 handling in speculative path is not robust/correct, acceptance logic is not properly per-batch, and there is no explicit EOS/stop handling (runs strictly max_new_tokens). Code is truncated so end-to-end runnability of the whole file/report section cannot be fully verified.
V. LLM Review (30pts).Overall comment: Good high-level structure (parallel target verify, residual resampling, KV slice rollback), but the implementation is not a correct lossless rejection sampler in the general case due to batchwise `.all()` accept/reject and a flawed temperature==0 acceptance path. Significant inefficiency remains from repeated `torch.cat` on the growing sequence tensor.

eval_report.comment: Good. Core algorithm is basically correct, with room for improvement.
